domain: Cherokee syllabary (Sequoyah's 12-year solo invention; 85 syllable-symbols; deliberately mid-granularity between alphabet and word; designed from scratch for Cherokee; achieved mass literacy in months)
specific_mechanism: Closed fixed-size syllable-granularity inventory designed FROM SCRATCH for one language; optimal-size sweet spot between too-small (ambiguity) and too-large (unmemorizable).
llm_application: SYLLABARY-VOCAB: a low-resource-language LLM tokenizer regime that (i) trains a DELIBERATELY SMALL fixed-size vocabulary (~100-300 tokens) at SYLLABLE granularity, (ii) designed FROM SCRATCH (not inherited from a multilingual base or BPE-trained on mixed data), (iii) co-trained with the model architecture choice, (iv) explicitly tied to the language's syllable inventory (one token per attested syllable + a small Unicode-fallback set). Different from BPE/SentencePiece (data-driven, variable granularity) and from byte-level (too fine) — explicitly *designed* mid-granularity inventory.
novelty_claim: BPE / SentencePiece / WordPiece / Unigram LM are data-driven tokenizers (no design intent). Byte-level (ByT5) is too fine. Char-level too fine. Word-level too coarse. Glyph (visual) is orthogonal. Language-specific tokenizers exist but typically inherit BPE methodology. SYLLABARY-VOCAB differs by EXPLICIT SYLLABLE-LEVEL HAND-CURATED MID-GRANULARITY INVENTORY designed from scratch + SMALL FIXED SIZE + EXPLICIT TIE TO PHONOLOGICAL SYLLABLE INVENTORY OF THE LANGUAGE.
